requests.reason: Current RFO FAR two-step sealed bidding procedure

guidance: Use current RFO FAR 14.211-3(a)/(a)(1), not legacy FAR 14.503-1, for what to do in step one of two-step sealed bidding.

requests.reason: RFO final indirect cost rate procedures
DoD final indirect cost PGI procedure

guidance: Use current RFO FAR 42.505(a)(1), not legacy FAR 42.705-1(b), for contracting officer final indirect cost rate determination procedures.
Route special contracting methods to RFO FAR Part 17.

requests.reason: Extraordinary contractual actions under current RFO Part 50

guidance: Do not recycle old FAR 50.103-5(c) as controlling if current RFO Part 50 text does not contain the old funds-availability prerequisite; mark the conclusion conditional when the current text is silent.
Use RFO FAR 36.102 for acquisition of architect-engineer services instead of legacy FAR subpart 36.6.

requests.reason: RFO responsibility and preaward survey baseline

guidance: For short-form preaward survey timing, do not cite old FAR 9.106-4(d). Current RFO FAR 9.106 is reserved; DoD PGI 209.171 explains how to request surveys, not when a short form is appropriate.

requests.reason: Tie bid procedure under sealed bidding

guidance: Use current RFO FAR 14.308(b) or RFO FAR 19.203 for equal low bids, not legacy FAR 14.408-6 or 19.202-3.

requests.reason: DoD workforce mix and acquisition planning overlay

guidance: For DoD, lead with DFARS RFO 237.301-1(a)(i); RFO FAR 37.301-1(a) and 7.503(a) are supporting baseline FAR context.
Answer no for performance and payment bonds in DoD cost-reimbursement construction contracts; DFARS RFO 228.102-1 waives the requirement.

requests.reason: Value engineering prescription and RFO status
Value engineering clause text containing decision timeframe

guidance: RFO FAR Part 48 does not itself state the time. The timing is in the Government action paragraph of the VE clauses: FAR 52.248-1(e)(1) for supplies/services and FAR 52.248-3(e)(1) for construction.

match: reviewed to calculate adjustment for inflation

requests.reason: FAR conventions and threshold adjustment baseline

guidance: DFARS RFO PGI 201.108 points to the threshold spreadsheet; the five-year answer is inferred from that review cycle rather than stated as a simple sentence.
Use DFARS RFO PGI 201.170-2(a) for pre-award peer review timing.
Use RFO FAR 28.307-2(a) for the required amount of employer's liability insurance under a cost-reimbursement contract.
In addition to name, title, and signature, include telephone number and, when available, email address; cite DFARS RFO PGI 204.101(1).
The instruction is DoD Instruction 1100.22; cite DFARS RFO 207.503(e)(i) for the DoD regulatory hook.

match: doesn't have to reach agreement on every element of cost
does not have to reach agreement on every element of cost
guidance: Use RFO FAR 15.405(a): the contracting officer need not agree on every cost element to negotiate a fair and reasonable price.
For DoD utilities privatization cost-principle relief, route to the class deviation answer CD 2011-O0006 / DoD RFO Deviation Part 31; mark source currency if the deviation PDF text is not retrieved.
Use DFARS RFO 208.7302 and PGI 208.73: DoD should furnish available required precious metals rather than have contractors acquire them independently when in the Government's best interest.
Use DFARS RFO 232.7002(a)(2) for the exception when electronic submission is not feasible.

match: offeror submit information about its political
guidance: For the prohibition on requiring offeror political-spending information in a solicitation, use class deviation CD 2012-O0010; mark deviation source UTR if not retrieved.

requests.reason: Contractor personnel performing in Japan class deviation text

guidance: Include DFARS 252.225-7976 when contractor personnel are required to perform in Japan; cite CD 2018-O0019 / DFARS RFO 225 text.
The maximum period for a personal services contract for temporary consultant services is one year; cite DFARS RFO 237.106(1)(i).
For purchases outside the United States in support of a humanitarian or peacekeeping operation, the SAT is $650,000 under FAR/RFO FAR 2.101.
For contracts performed entirely in Tonga, answer no; use current RFO FAR 19.109(b)(3), not legacy FAR 19.702(b)(3).

requests.reason: FAR conventions for application of changes

guidance: If the final rule is silent, the contracting officer does not have to amend the existing solicitation; cite FAR/RFO FAR 1.108(d).

requests.title: Class Deviation 2021-O0008 Revision 1 - Limitations on Subcontracting for Small Business

guidance: For the noncommercial supply small-business set-aside scenario, the expected result under the identified deviation pathway is no under CD 2021-O0008 Revision 1; mark deviation source currency if not retrieved.
A contractor can submit a claim by email; use current RFO FAR 33.205-1(b), not legacy FAR 33.206(a).

match: delegated to the heads of contracting activities
guidance: Use FAR/RFO FAR 1.108(b): agency-head authority may be delegated unless the FAR says otherwise.

requests.reason: DoD cooperative agreement holder clause prescription

guidance: Use DFARS RFO 205.470. Evaluate total contemplated value including options, not just the IDIQ guaranteed minimum.
Yes, the contracting officer must consult with the program manager and fee determining official when developing the award-fee plan; cite DFARS RFO PGI 216.401(e)(i).
When FAR 6.302-7(c)(2) says notification is required without naming a different actor, use FAR/RFO FAR 1.108(f); the contracting officer is responsible.
Do not include FAR 52.241-2 for telecommunications services inside the United States; cite RFO FAR 41.102(b)(3).
Compute the deadline as September 5, 2023. In RFO mode cite RFO FAR 15.206-2(a)(1); legacy FAR crosswalk references may show FAR 15.505(a)(1).

requests.reason: DoD contractor identification of sources of supply

guidance: For noncommercial supplies valued at $350,000, answer no if the DFARS 217.7302(b)(2) exception applies.
For award to an AbilityOne vendor, answer no for documenting FAPIIS consideration in a responsibility determination; cite RFO FAR 9.102(b)(3).

requests.reason: Contractor personnel in designated operational areas

guidance: For contractor personnel carrying weapons in a designated operational area during a peacekeeping operation, answer yes; cite RFO FAR 25.301-3.

requests.reason: Full and open competition scope exception

guidance: No FAR 6.303 justification is required when using simplified acquisition procedures; cite FAR/RFO FAR 6.001(a).

requests.reason: DoD Government property accountability transfer procedure

guidance: Use DFARS RFO PGI 245.103-71 for procedures for transferring accountability for Government property.
Do not require DFARS PGI 245.103-70 for office space, desks, and phones in the co-location scenario; cite FAR/RFO FAR 45.000(b)(5).
Answer no because $15,000 does not exceed the micro-purchase threshold; cite FAR/RFO FAR 25.100(b)(1).
Answer no when the cancellation ceiling is exactly $20 million; the rule triggers above/exceeding the threshold, not equal to it.
For an unclassified DoD construction contract, the Government estimate is designated For Official Use Only; cite DFARS RFO PGI 236.203(1).

match: leased for a period of 60 days
guidance: Answer no in the 60-day overseas motor-vehicle lease scenario because RFO FAR 8.1100 is the scope gate for the certification requirement.
Yes, the HCA authority may be delegated unless the FAR says otherwise; cite RFO FAR 1.108(b).
Answer generally prohibited, but the Secretary of Defense may waive the prohibition for 60 days; cite the current DFARS RFO 237.873-4 waiver rule and supporting prohibition at 237.873-3(a).

match: first time this item has been purchased by the dod
not solicit from nontraditional defense contractors
guidance: Answer true: a written determination is required for the stated DoD first-time FAR Part 12 supply procurement above the SAT when not using the nontraditional-defense-contractor path.

requests.reason: RFO FAR Part 27 DoD scope gate
DoD technical data overlay and FAR 27.4 redirect

guidance: For the identified DoD issue-family pathway, the likely determination is no: FAR 52.227-14 is not required solely under FAR 27.409(b)(1) because FAR/RFO FAR 27.400 and DFARS/RFO DFARS 227.400 redirect DoD to DFARS 227.71/227.72.

requests.reason: UCA obligation limitation and initial-spares exception

guidance: Answer no for a UCA for initial spares; DFARS RFO 217.7404-5(a) excludes initial spares from the normal obligation limits.
Answer no. For DoD fixed-price construction performed in Germany, DFARS RFO 246.710(2) requires 252.246-7002 instead of FAR 52.246-21.
For contractor personnel in the USCENTCOM AOR, use CD 2017-O0004 / DFARS RFO 225.371-5 class-deviation text in lieu of the baseline DFARS 252.225-7040 clause.

match: service contract for a military flight simulator
guidance: Answer no: a DoD activity cannot enter into a service contract for a military flight simulator without a Secretary of Defense waiver; cite DFARS RFO 237.102-71(b).

requests.reason: Subcontractor size and socioeconomic representation rule

guidance: Answer yes: the prime may accept the subcontractor's current, accurate, and complete written representation; cite RFO FAR 19.703(a)(2)(i).

requests.reason: DoD assignment of claims nonpersonal-services rule

guidance: For a nonpersonal services contract, assignment of claims may not be prohibited; DFARS RFO 232.803(b) says only personal services contracts may prohibit assignment.
Answer no: an affirmative responsibility determination is not required before award to an AbilityOne participating nonprofit agency; cite RFO FAR 9.102(b)(3).
